hypothesis: AMDEN, an EGNN-based diffusion model, can generate amorphous materials—including multi-element glasses—whose radial distribution functions, bond angle distributions, structure factors, and potential energy distributions closely match MD-generated reference structures, and its energy-based denoising with HMC refinement produces samples with lower potential energy and improved structural fidelity relative to standard reverse-SDE denoising while classifier-free guidance enables conditioning on Young's modulus for inverse design.
